solution: 
```python
class Solution:
    def maxSubstrings(self, word: str) -> int:
        count = 0
        i = 0
        while i < len(word) - 3:
            if word[i] == word[i + 3]:
                count += 1
                i += 4  # Move past the current valid substring
            else:
                i += 1
        return count
```
efficiency: MEDIUM
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: <The algorithm scans the string from left to right, checking for substrings of at least four characters that start and end with the same character. It increments the count and skips ahead by four characters to avoid overlapping substrings. This greedy approach is efficient but may miss some optimal combinations if longer substrings exist.>
